claimText: Note: Constitution of India (under Article 163 (2)) explicitly mentions that in certain spheres the Governor of a State acts in his/her own discretion. However, Constitution does not empower President to independently (suo motu) take up a State bill unless it has first been reserved and forwarded by the Governor.
